page.content: The table presents data on various re-included subpopulations in clinical trials, detailing their natural augmentation, prediction bias, and mean squared error (MSE) of prediction. For the row labeled "Relaxed eligibility criteria," there were 188 patients re-included with a prediction bias of 0.033 and an MSE of 0.818. The "1 past suicide attempt" row shows 190 patients re-included, a prediction bias of 0.057, and an MSE of 0.820. In the "Private practice" category, 159 patients were re-included with a prediction bias of 0.041 and an MSE of 0.830. The "Alcohol abuse" row included 73 patients with a prediction bias of 0.054 and an MSE of 0.836, while "Drug abuse" had 65 patients re-included, a prediction bias of 0.053, and an MSE of 0.833. The combination of "Illness duration between 1 and 3 years + 1 past suicide attempt" resulted in 339 patients, a prediction bias of 0.024, and an MSE of 0.803. For "Illness duration between 1 and 3 years + private practice," there were 321 patients, a prediction bias of 0.024, and an MSE of 0.814.

page.content: The "Illness duration between 1 and 3 years + alcohol abuse" included 248 patients, a prediction bias of 0.037, and an MSE of 0.812. The "RCT population" is not applicable with a prediction bias of 0.054 and an MSE of 0.852. Lastly, the "SOHO 'real-world population'" row is also not applicable, with a prediction bias of 0.000 and an MSE of 0.000.
